title: rubus × planitiae
intro: Rubus × planitiae, also known as the hybrid blackberry or the Hall's blackberry, is a hybrid plant species that combines characteristics from the European blackberry (Rubus fruticosus) and the Himalayan blackberry (Rubus armeniacus). The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × planitiae are attributed to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Rubus × planitiae can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used as a jam or syrup ingredient due to its sweet and slightly tart flavor.
